memory_safety.description: Rust's ownership system prevents use-after-free, double-free, and buffer overflows at compile time.
memory_safety.notes: Memory safety prevents memory corruption but does not prevent logic-level XSS; output encoding is still required.

type_safety.description: Strong static type system prevents type-confusion attacks and enforces safe data handling.
type_safety.notes: Type safety alone does not prevent XSS; template engines still need context-aware escaping.

borrow_checking.description: The borrow checker prevents data races and aliasing bugs at compile time.
borrow_checking.notes: Primarily a concurrency safety property with secondary security benefits.

memory_safety.description: Garbage-collected runtime with no manual memory management; prevents most memory corruption.
memory_safety.notes: Memory safety reduces certain exploit classes but XSS remains a logic-level concern.

type_safety.description: Static type system prevents type-confusion attacks; interfaces provide safe dynamic dispatch.
type_safety.notes: Go html/template provides contextual auto-escaping (detected separately as framework evidence).

type_safety.description: Static type checking catches type errors at compile time; types are erased at runtime.
type_safety.notes: TypeScript types are erased at runtime; XSS protection comes from framework encoding, not types.

strict_null_checks.description: strictNullChecks prevents null/undefined reference errors at compile time.
strict_null_checks.notes: Prevents 'undefined is not a function' class bugs but does not directly prevent XSS.

dynamic_typing.description: Dynamic typing allows flexible code but requires explicit input validation.
dynamic_typing.notes: Dynamic typing means type and shape validation must be explicit at runtime.

memory_safety.description: Garbage-collected runtime with no manual memory management.
memory_safety.notes: Memory safety reduces certain exploit classes but XSS remains a logic-level concern.

dynamic_typing.description: Dynamic typing allows flexible code but requires explicit input validation.
dynamic_typing.notes: Dynamic typing means type validation must be explicit; use type hints and runtime validators.

memory_safety.description: Garbage-collected runtime with no manual memory management.
memory_safety.notes: Memory safety reduces certain exploit classes but XSS remains a logic-level concern.

dynamic_typing.description: Dynamic typing with coercive type juggling; requires explicit input validation.
dynamic_typing.notes: PHP type juggling can produce unexpected behavior; always validate input types explicitly.

memory_safety.description: Garbage-collected runtime with no manual memory management.
memory_safety.notes: Memory safety reduces certain exploit classes but XSS remains a logic-level concern.

dynamic_typing.description: Dynamic typing with duck typing; requires explicit input validation.
dynamic_typing.notes: Ruby's flexibility means validation must be explicit; Rails provides helpers (detected separately).

memory_safety.description: JVM-managed memory with no manual memory management; type-safe bytecode.
memory_safety.notes: JVM memory safety prevents memory corruption but XSS/SSRF/etc. remain logic concerns.

type_safety.description: Strong static type system prevents type-confusion attacks.
type_safety.notes: Type safety prevents many injection classes but application-level encoding is still required.

memory_safety.description: .NET managed runtime with garbage collection; prevents most memory corruption.
memory_safety.notes: Managed memory prevents buffer overflows but application-level XSS still possible.

type_safety.description: Strong static type system with generics prevents type-confusion attacks.
type_safety.notes: System.Web.HttpUtility.HtmlEncode and Razor auto-escaping provide additional protection.

memory_safety.description: JVM-managed memory with null safety guarantees.
memory_safety.notes: JVM-level safety plus Kotlin null safety prevents many bug classes.

type_safety.description: Strong static type system with null safety prevents type and null-pointer errors.
type_safety.notes: Null safety eliminates NPE attacks but application-level encoding is still required.

memory_safety.description: Automatic Reference Counting (ARC) with bounds checking; no manual memory management.
memory_safety.notes: Swift is memory-safe by default; XSS is primarily a web-layer concern.

type_safety.description: Strong static type system with optionals for null safety.
type_safety.notes: Swift's strict typing eliminates many injection vectors at the language level.

memory_safety.description: BEAM VM with immutable data and process isolation; no shared memory between processes.
memory_safety.notes: BEAM process isolation provides strong memory safety guarantees.

functional_guards.description: Immutable data and pattern matching reduce state-based vulnerabilities.
functional_guards.notes: Immutability helps but does not prevent XSS; Phoenix HEEx auto-escapes (detected separately).

memory_safety.description: JVM-managed memory with strong functional programming guarantees.
memory_safety.notes: JVM-level safety plus functional patterns reduce state-based vulnerabilities.

type_safety.description: Rich static type system with pattern matching and exhaustive checking.
type_safety.notes: Scala's expressive type system can encode invariants but XSS still requires encoding.

memory_safety.description: Garbage-collected runtime with sound null safety.
memory_safety.notes: Null safety prevents NPE-based attacks but XSS is a web-layer concern.

type_safety.description: Sound static type system with null safety.
type_safety.notes: Dart's sound type system eliminates many type-based injection vectors.

memory_safety.description: JVM-managed memory with immutable persistent data structures.
memory_safety.notes: Immutability reduces state-based vulnerabilities but XSS still requires encoding.

dynamic_typing.description: Dynamic typing with gradual typing support via clojure.spec.
dynamic_typing.notes: Clojure's dynamic nature means input validation should be explicit.

template.notes: Template files (HTML, HBS, etc.) have no language-level security properties. All protection comes from the template engine.

graphql.notes: GraphQL schema files have no runtime security properties. Protection comes from the server framework.

markdown.notes: Markdown files have no language-level security properties. Protection comes from the renderer.
